title: Quiz: Quantum Eigenvalue Algorithms
description: Test your understanding of QPE variants, variational eigensolvers, and spectral analysis techniques

questions.question: What does standard Quantum Phase Estimation output?

options.text: A binary representation of the phase θ where eigenvalue is e^{2πiθ}
The condition number of the operator

questions.explanation: QPE extracts the phase θ encoded in the eigenvalue e^{2πiθ} and stores it as an n-bit binary fraction in the precision register. The eigenstate must be provided as input.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of Iterative QPE over standard QPE?

options.text: Uses only one ancilla qubit instead of n precision qubits

questions.explanation: Iterative QPE dramatically reduces qubit requirements by using only one precision qubit and extracting bits sequentially through repeated measurements. This trades qubits for circuit repetitions.

questions.question: What does VQE optimize to find the ground state energy?

options.text: The overlap with a reference state

questions.explanation: VQE uses the variational principle: the ground state energy is the minimum of ⟨ψ|H|ψ⟩ over all states. By parameterizing the state as |ψ(θ)⟩ and minimizing over θ, VQE finds an upper bound to the ground state energy.

questions.question: Why is the spectral gap important for quantum eigensolvers?

options.text: It sets the minimum precision needed to distinguish eigenvalues
It affects the number of qubits required

questions.explanation: The spectral gap Δ = min|λᵢ - λⱼ| determines the precision ε < Δ/2 needed to distinguish different eigenvalues. Smaller gaps require more precision qubits in QPE or more optimization iterations in VQE.

questions.question: VQE can only find the ground state energy, not excited state energies.
questions.explanation: False! While basic VQE targets the ground state, variants like VQE with deflation, SSVQE (Subspace-Search VQE), and qEOM (quantum equation of motion) can find excited states. Quantum subspace methods also target multiple eigenvalues.

questions.question: What is the Quantum Krylov subspace method?

options.text: A method that builds subspace {|ψ⟩, H|ψ⟩, H²|ψ⟩, ...} to approximate eigenvalues

questions.explanation: Quantum Krylov methods build a subspace by repeatedly applying the Hamiltonian to an initial state. The effective Hamiltonian in this subspace is classically diagonalized to find approximate eigenvalues.

questions.question: How does the number of precision qubits n affect QPE accuracy?

options.text: More qubits decrease accuracy due to noise

questions.explanation: With n precision qubits, QPE estimates phases to n bits, giving resolution 1/2^n. The discretization error decreases exponentially with n, though noise effects may counteract this in practice.

questions.question: Which are valid error sources in VQE? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Statistical shot noise from finite measurements
Classical optimizer getting stuck in local minima
Ansatz not being expressive enough to represent ground state

questions.explanation: VQE errors come from: (a) finite sampling when measuring expectation values, (b) optimization landscape issues including barren plateaus and local minima, (c) ansatz expressibility limitations. QFT discretization (d) is a QPE error, not VQE.
